hero.title: Future Agriculture Starts Here
hero.subtitle: We build intelligent farms powered by technology, data, and innovation.
hero.imageAlt: Real NASA satellite imagery of the AlUla region, Saudi Arabia

about.title: Cultivating the future of farming
about.lead: PURE LINE is an agricultural technology company transforming traditional farms into intelligent, data-driven operations.
about.whoText: A team of agronomists, engineers and data scientists building end-to-end smart farming systems for the modern Gulf.
about.whatText: We design, develop and operate smart farms — from IoT sensor networks and precision irrigation to satellite monitoring and AI analytics.
about.expertiseText: A decade of field experience across arid-climate agriculture, greenhouse engineering and agricultural digital transformation.
about.missionText: To make every farm more productive, more sustainable and fully connected — feeding the future with less water and less waste.

services.title: Complete smart-farming solutions
services.subtitle: Twelve integrated services covering the full lifecycle of a modern farm.

smart.title: IoT Sensor Installation
smart.desc: IoT-connected farms with real-time sensing, automation and AI-driven decisions.
smart.heroDesc: A dense network of soil, climate and crop sensors giving you real-time, field-level visibility.
smart.fullDescription: We design and install IoT sensor networks — soil moisture and EC probes, weather stations, canopy sensors and flow meters — that stream live data to the PURE LINE platform. Automated alerts and historical logging turn raw readings into decisions your team can act on immediately.
benefits: Real-time visibility into soil, climate and water conditions
Automated alerts before conditions become critical
Historical data logging for season-over-season comparison
Low-power, solar-ready hardware for remote fields
specs: Soil moisture, EC and temperature probes
On-site weather stations (temp, humidity, wind, rainfall)
Flow meters and pressure sensors for irrigation lines
Solar power kits for off-grid installations
deliverables: Sensor network design and placement plan
Live dashboard access with historical logs
smart.pricing: Priced per sensor node and gateway coverage area — request a quote based on your field layout.

faq.q: How long do sensor batteries or solar kits last?
faq.a: Solar-powered nodes run indefinitely with routine panel cleaning; battery-only nodes typically last 12-18 months between replacements.

faq.q: What's the wireless range of the sensors?
faq.a: LoRaWAN gateways typically cover 3-8km in open desert terrain, meaning one gateway can often serve an entire farm.

faq.q: Can sensors trigger irrigation automatically?
faq.a: Yes, when paired with our Smart Irrigation service, soil-moisture thresholds can trigger automated valve activation.

faq.q: What happens if a sensor fails?
faq.a: The dashboard flags offline or out-of-range sensors automatically, and our support team schedules replacement or recalibration.

development.title: Feasibility Studies
development.desc: Rigorous technical and economic feasibility studies before you commit capital to a new agricultural project.
development.heroDesc: Rigorous technical and economic feasibility studies before you commit capital to a new agricultural project.
development.fullDescription: Before breaking ground, our feasibility studies evaluate soil and water resources, climate suitability, crop selection, infrastructure requirements and projected returns — giving investors and operators a clear, evidence-based go/no-go decision and a roadmap for execution.
benefits: Evidence-based go/no-go decision before capital is committed
Realistic cost, yield and return projections
Identification of water, soil or regulatory risks early
A clear implementation roadmap if the project proceeds
development.pricing: Custom quote based on project scale and study depth — from rapid desk assessments to full investment-grade studies.

faq.q: How long does a feasibility study take?
faq.a: A standard study takes 4-8 weeks depending on site access, data availability and study depth.

faq.q: Can this be used to support financing applications?
faq.a: Yes, our studies are structured to support investor and lender due diligence, with financial projections included.

faq.q: What if the study concludes the project isn't viable?
faq.a: We report findings honestly — a negative or conditional result at this stage saves significant capital versus discovering issues after construction.

faq.q: Do you also handle the resulting project construction?
faq.a: Yes, PURE LINE can carry projects from feasibility through greenhouse construction, irrigation and platform deployment.

irrigation.title: Smart Irrigation
irrigation.desc: Precision drip and smart irrigation that cut water use while boosting yield.
irrigation.heroDesc: Automated, sensor-driven irrigation that delivers exactly the right amount of water, exactly when your crops need it.
irrigation.fullDescription: PURE LINE designs and installs smart irrigation systems that combine soil-moisture sensing, weather data and automated valves to cut water consumption while improving yield consistency. From drip networks in open fields to fertigation-ready greenhouse loops, every system is engineered for AlUla's arid climate and can be monitored and adjusted remotely from the PURE LINE platform.
benefits: Reduce water consumption by up to 40% compared with flood irrigation
Deliver nutrients precisely through fertigation-ready lines
Remote scheduling and manual override from any device
Fewer labor hours spent on manual valve operation
specs: Soil-moisture and EC sensors at configurable depths
Solar or mains-powered automated valve controllers
deliverables: Site-specific irrigation design and hydraulic plan
Mobile/dashboard access to scheduling and history
irrigation.pricing: Priced per hectare based on field layout, water source and automation level — request a free site assessment for a firm quotation.

faq.q: How much water can I actually save?
faq.a: Most AlUla farms see 25-40% reduction in water use versus flood irrigation, depending on crop type and existing infrastructure.

faq.q: Can the system work with my existing well or pivot?
faq.a: Yes. We integrate with existing water sources, pumps and pivots wherever possible to avoid unnecessary rebuilding.

faq.q: Do I need internet access on the farm?
faq.a: No. Sites without reliable connectivity use LoRa or scheduled offline controllers that sync when a connection is available.

faq.q: How long does installation take?
faq.a: A typical 5-10 hectare system is designed and installed within 3-6 weeks, depending on field access and material lead times.

greenhouse.title: Greenhouse Solutions
greenhouse.desc: Climate-controlled greenhouses engineered for year-round high-value production.
greenhouse.heroDesc: Climate-controlled greenhouse structures engineered for AlUla's heat, built for reliable year-round production.
greenhouse.fullDescription: We design, build and commission greenhouses ranging from simple shade-net structures to fully climate-controlled glasshouses with fogging, ventilation and CO2 enrichment. Every greenhouse is engineered for the local climate and integrated with our irrigation and sensor systems for a single connected operation.
benefits: Grow high-value crops year-round regardless of outside temperature
Cut water and pesticide use through a controlled microclimate
Extend growing seasons and increase cycles per year
Integrated with irrigation, sensors and remote monitoring
specs: Galvanized steel or aluminium structural frames
Evaporative cooling, fogging and forced ventilation options
Structural design rated for local wind loads
greenhouse.pricing: Custom quote based on greenhouse type, footprint and climate-control specification.

faq.q: What crops work best in an AlUla greenhouse?
faq.a: Leafy greens, tomatoes, peppers, cucumbers and strawberries perform particularly well under controlled cooling and shading.

faq.q: How hot does it get inside during summer?
faq.a: With properly sized evaporative cooling and ventilation, internal temperatures are typically kept 8-15°C below ambient peak temperatures.

faq.q: Can an existing structure be retrofitted?
faq.a: In many cases yes — we assess existing structures for retrofitting with cooling, sensors and irrigation before recommending a full rebuild.

faq.q: What's the typical build timeline?
faq.a: A mid-size commercial greenhouse (1,000-5,000 m²) typically takes 8-14 weeks from approved design to handover.

consulting.title: Agricultural Consultancy
consulting.desc: Expert agronomy, feasibility and optimization studies grounded in data.
consulting.heroDesc: Independent agronomic and technical advice to plan, troubleshoot or optimize your farming operation.
consulting.fullDescription: Our consultancy team pairs decades of arid-climate agronomy experience with the same satellite, soil and sensor data used across our other services, giving you independent, data-backed recommendations — whether you're planning a new farm, troubleshooting yield issues, or optimizing an existing operation.
benefits: Independent, data-backed recommendations, not just opinion
Access to agronomists with regional arid-climate experience
Findings grounded in your farm's actual satellite and soil data
Actionable reports, not just verbal advice
specs: Soil, water and crop diagnostic assessment
Written advisory report with prioritized recommendations
deliverables: Site visit and/or remote data review
consulting.pricing: Custom quote based on scope — single-issue consultations, full farm audits and ongoing retainers are all available.

faq.q: What kind of problems can consultancy help with?
faq.a: Common cases include unexplained yield decline, irrigation inefficiency, soil salinity concerns, and evaluating new crop or technology investments.

faq.q: Do you consult on farms that aren't PURE LINE clients?
faq.a: Yes, consultancy is available independently of our other services, though existing clients benefit from richer historical data.

faq.q: Is a site visit required?
faq.a: Many assessments can start remotely using satellite and sensor data, with an on-site visit recommended for physical soil or infrastructure issues.

faq.q: How long until I receive the report?
faq.a: Standard advisory reports are delivered within 10 business days of the site visit or data review.

digital.title: Farm Management Platform
digital.desc: Bring legacy farms online with dashboards, analytics and connected operations.
digital.heroDesc: One connected dashboard bringing irrigation, satellite, sensor and yield data into a single farm operations view.
digital.fullDescription: The PURE LINE Farm Management Platform unifies every data stream — irrigation controllers, soil sensors, satellite NDVI, weather and yield records — into a single web dashboard. Farm managers get real-time visibility and historical reporting without juggling multiple disconnected systems.
benefits: Single login for irrigation, sensors, satellite and weather data
Real-time alerts across every connected system
Role-based access for owners, managers and field staff
Exportable reports for stakeholders and lenders
specs: Web dashboard, responsive on mobile and desktop
API integrations with common sensor/controller brands
Configurable alert rules and notification channels
deliverables: Configured platform account for your farm(s)
Integration of existing sensors/controllers where possible
digital.pricing: Subscription tier based on number of farms, users and connected devices — request a quote to size your plan.

faq.q: Can I connect equipment I already own?
faq.a: We support integration with many common irrigation controllers and sensor brands; our team will confirm compatibility during onboarding.

faq.q: Is the platform available in Arabic?
faq.a: Yes, the platform fully supports both English and Arabic, including right-to-left layout.

faq.q: How many users can access one farm account?
faq.a: Plans include multiple role-based users by default, with additional seats available as needed.

faq.q: Is my farm data private?
faq.a: Yes — all farm data is scoped to your account and is never shared with other customers.

satelliteMonitoring.title: Satellite Farm Analysis
satelliteMonitoring.desc: Continuous multispectral tracking of crop health across every field from orbit.
satelliteMonitoring.heroDesc: Full-farm satellite analysis combining imagery, field boundaries and health metrics into one intelligence report.
satelliteMonitoring.fullDescription: Satellite Farm Analysis goes beyond a single index — we combine high-resolution imagery, precise field-boundary mapping and multi-band analysis to deliver a complete picture of farm health, change detection and asset inventory, refreshed on a regular capture cycle.
benefits: Whole-farm visibility without physical inspection of every field
Change detection flags new stress or damage automatically
Accurate field boundaries for compliance and planning
Historical imagery archive for every monitored farm
specs: Field boundary extraction and polygon mapping
satelliteMonitoring.pricing: Custom quote based on farm acreage, imagery resolution and capture frequency.

faq.q: What resolution imagery do you use?
faq.a: We use a mix of commercial high-resolution optical imagery and regional multispectral sources, selected per project requirements and budget.

faq.q: Can you map fields that were never surveyed on the ground?
faq.a: Yes — field boundaries can be extracted directly from imagery and refined with light ground verification.

faq.q: How is change detection different from NDVI trend?
faq.a: Change detection compares two dates directly to flag new damage, land-use change or clearing, while NDVI trend tracks gradual vigor changes over time.

faq.q: Is historical imagery available for my farm?
faq.a: For farms in our monitored regions we retain a multi-year archive; for new sites, the archive begins building from your first capture.

ndviServices.title: NDVI Monitoring
ndviServices.desc: Vegetation-index mapping that reveals stress and vigor long before the eye can.
ndviServices.heroDesc: Satellite-derived NDVI tracking that reveals crop stress and vigor long before it's visible to the eye.
ndviServices.fullDescription: Our NDVI Monitoring service processes multispectral satellite imagery into vegetation-index maps for every field, updated on a regular revisit cycle. Trend lines, anomaly detection and status alerts give your team an early warning system for water stress, disease and nutrient deficiency.
benefits: Detect crop stress 1-3 weeks before it's visible on the ground
Track vegetation trends over 12- and 36-month windows
Prioritize field visits to the areas that need attention most
Benchmark performance across seasons and fields
specs: NDVI derived from multispectral satellite bands
deliverables: NDVI heatmap for every registered field
Trend history and downloadable PDF reports
Access via the PURE LINE dashboard
ndviServices.pricing: Subscription priced per hectare monitored, with volume discounts for multi-farm operations — request a quote for your acreage.

faq.q: How accurate is satellite NDVI compared to ground sensors?
faq.a: NDVI is a strong proxy for canopy vigor and correlates well with ground truth, though we recommend pairing it with soil sensors for root-zone conditions.

faq.q: How often is imagery refreshed?
faq.a: Standard plans refresh weekly to bi-weekly depending on satellite revisit and cloud cover; higher-frequency tasking is available on request.

faq.q: Can I monitor multiple farms from one account?
faq.a: Yes, the dashboard supports unlimited registered fields and lets you compare farms side by side.

faq.q: What do I do when an alert fires?
faq.a: Alerts flag the affected zone and severity so your field team can prioritize a ground inspection; our agronomists can also advise on likely causes.

precisionAg.title: Precision Agriculture
precisionAg.desc: Variable-rate inputs and zone management driven by real field data.
precisionAg.heroDesc: Zone-by-zone precision farming that applies the right input, in the right place, at the right time.
precisionAg.fullDescription: Precision Agriculture combines field mapping, soil sampling and satellite data to divide your land into management zones, then drives variable-rate application of water, fertilizer and seed to match each zone's actual needs — reducing waste and improving consistency across the field.
benefits: Cut fertilizer and input costs by targeting only what each zone needs
Improve yield uniformity across variable soil conditions
Data-backed decisions replace blanket, farm-wide guesswork
Full audit trail of every input application
specs: Zone mapping from soil sampling + NDVI overlays
Variable-rate application maps (VRA) for compatible equipment
deliverables: Management zone map for your farm
precisionAg.pricing: Custom quote based on farm size, zone complexity and existing equipment compatibility.

faq.q: Do I need new equipment for variable-rate application?
faq.a: Most modern GPS-guided spreaders, planters and sprayers can read our prescription files directly; we can advise on retrofitting older equipment.

faq.q: How are management zones determined?
faq.a: We combine soil sampling, elevation data and multi-season NDVI history to identify zones with genuinely different input needs.

faq.q: Does this work for smaller farms?
faq.a: Yes — even 5-10 hectare farms benefit from zone management, though the cost-per-hectare is more favorable above 20 hectares.

faq.q: How often are zone maps updated?
faq.a: We recommend refreshing zone maps every 1-2 seasons, or sooner if crop rotation or soil amendments significantly change field conditions.

farmMapping.title: Water Management
farmMapping.desc: Full-cycle water management from source assessment to distribution efficiency.
farmMapping.heroDesc: Full-cycle water management — from source assessment to distribution efficiency and consumption tracking.
farmMapping.fullDescription: Water Management covers the complete water cycle on your farm: assessing source capacity (well, canal or municipal), auditing distribution efficiency, and implementing metering and controls that reduce waste while keeping crops properly supplied — critical in AlUla's arid, water-scarce environment.
benefits: Understand true water availability and seasonal risk
Identify and fix losses in distribution networks
Metered, auditable water consumption per field
Compliance-ready reporting for water-use regulations
specs: Distribution network pressure and loss audit
Flow metering at farm and zone level
deliverables: Water source and capacity assessment report
Distribution audit with identified loss points
farmMapping.pricing: Custom quote based on water source complexity and farm size.

faq.q: Can you assess a well I already have?
faq.a: Yes, we test yield, drawdown and water quality for existing wells as part of the assessment.

faq.q: What counts as a loss point in distribution?
faq.a: Common losses include leaking joints, pressure imbalance across zones, and unmetered or unmonitored offtakes — all of which we identify and quantify.

faq.q: Does this include groundwater regulation compliance?
faq.a: We help structure metering and reporting to align with regional water-use regulations, though final compliance sign-off remains with the relevant authority.

faq.q: How does this relate to Smart Irrigation?
faq.a: Water Management addresses the source-to-field water budget; Smart Irrigation optimizes how that water is applied at the plant level — the two are often deployed together.

gis.title: Climate Monitoring
gis.desc: On-farm weather stations and climate analytics tied to irrigation and crop decisions.
gis.heroDesc: On-farm weather stations and climate analytics that connect local conditions to irrigation and crop decisions.
gis.fullDescription: Climate Monitoring installs on-site weather stations and combines their readings with regional forecast data to give your team accurate, farm-specific climate intelligence — driving smarter irrigation timing, frost/heat alerts and season planning.
benefits: Farm-specific readings, not regional airport estimates
Early alerts for frost, heat stress and high wind events
Better-timed irrigation and spray operations
Historical climate log for season planning
gis.pricing: Priced per weather station and coverage area — request a quote for multi-station farm coverage.

faq.q: How many stations does my farm need?
faq.a: Most single-site farms need one station; larger or topographically varied farms may need multiple stations for accurate zone-level data.

faq.q: Can climate data trigger automated actions?
faq.a: Yes, when paired with Smart Irrigation or the Farm Management Platform, climate thresholds can trigger irrigation holds or alerts automatically.

faq.q: How accurate are frost alerts?
faq.a: On-site sensors typically detect frost risk more reliably than regional forecasts, since they measure actual microclimate conditions at crop height.

faq.q: Is historical data retained if I cancel the service?
faq.a: You can export your full historical data log at any time, including prior to cancellation.

remoteSensing.title: Custom Agricultural Projects
remoteSensing.desc: Bespoke agricultural technology projects engineered for unique requirements.
remoteSensing.heroDesc: Bespoke agricultural technology projects for requirements that don't fit a standard package.
remoteSensing.fullDescription: Some projects need a combination of services, custom engineering, or entirely new capability. Custom Agricultural Projects is our design-build track for large estates, research programs, and unique operational challenges — scoped, engineered and delivered by the same team behind our standard service lines.
benefits: A single accountable team for multi-discipline projects
Solutions engineered for your exact requirements
Access to the full PURE LINE technology stack as building blocks
Dedicated project management from scope to handover
specs: Custom scope combining any PURE LINE service lines
Dedicated project manager and technical lead
remoteSensing.pricing: Fully custom quote based on project scope — contact us to schedule a scoping consultation.

faq.q: What kind of projects fall under this service?
faq.a: Examples include multi-farm rollouts, research-station instrumentation, and integrations combining greenhouse, irrigation, satellite and platform work into one program.

faq.q: How is pricing determined without a fixed package?
faq.a: We scope the project in detail first — equipment, labor, software and timeline — then issue a fixed-price or milestone-based quotation.

faq.q: Can you work alongside our existing contractors or engineers?
faq.a: Yes, we regularly coordinate with client-side teams and other contractors on larger programs.

faq.q: What's the first step to start a custom project?
faq.a: Submit a request through our Service Request Portal describing your goals, and our team will schedule a scoping consultation.

footer.desc: PURE LINE is an agricultural technology company building intelligent, sustainable farms powered by data and innovation.

step1.title: Consultation & Site Assessment
step1.desc: We review your farm's goals, location and constraints, either remotely or with an on-site visit.

step2.title: Design & Proposal
step2.desc: Our engineers and agronomists design a tailored solution and issue a detailed scope and quotation.

step3.title: Implementation
step3.desc: Certified technicians deploy the system, following the approved design and project timeline.

step4.title: Testing & Calibration
step4.desc: Every system is tested, calibrated and validated against performance targets before handover.

step5.title: Ongoing Support
step5.desc: We monitor performance and provide maintenance, upgrades and agronomic support long after go-live.

servicesMarketplace.subtitle: Browse our full catalogue of smart-farming services and open any card for full details, pricing guidance and FAQs.
